compiled_state.title: [sprint-next-batch/extract_files]: extension-less files invisible to overlap detector -> SKIPPED_OVERLAP never emitted for tasks sharing file
compiled_state.author: Worktree
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
sprint-next-batch.sh's extract_files() requires a file extension to detect file paths in task titles and descriptions. Both the backtick regex (`...\.\w+`) and the prose-path regex (\.(?:py|sh|md|json|yaml|toml)) require an extension. Files without extensions — most notably plugins/dso/scripts/ticket (the main dispatcher) — are invisible to the overlap detector.

## Impact
Two tasks that both modify an extension-less file will not be serialized as SKIPPED_OVERLAP. For plugins/dso/scripts/ticket (high fan-in, frequently modified), this means concurrent agent batches could produce merge conflicts that the file-overlap serialization was designed to prevent.

## Root Cause
extract_files() in plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-next-batch.sh line 386. Both extraction paths require a file extension:
- Backtick path: `([^`]+\.\w+)`
- Prose path: plugins/**/*.{py,sh,md,json,yaml,toml}

Extension-less files (plugins/dso/scripts/ticket, plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh equivalent without extension, etc.) are never added to the conflict set.

## Suggested Fix
Add a separate extraction pass for known extension-less dispatcher files, or extend the backtick regex to accept paths without extensions when they match a known directory root (plugins/, .claude/, etc.).

## Discovered During
Sprint 2c28-e5b9 — writing SKIPPED_OVERLAP integration test. The test fixture initially used plugins/dso/scripts/ticket as the shared file and no overlap was detected.

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 2 (BASIC). Severity=1, Complexity=1 (single function), Environment=0, Intermittent=0.
comments.author: Worktree
